meta.title: Te Pā Governance Graph
meta.subtitle: Reflexive systems audit of Te Pā's own decision-making, feedback loops, and pathway growth
meta.kaupapa: Built under the Māori Data Governance Model of Te Kāhui Raraunga. Every node, loop, decision route, and comparison axis cites a public evidence source — repo file, GitHub Discussions thread, government page, or published governance document.
meta.provenance_note: Evidence references follow the same convention as the observatory's indicator sources: only public, forkable, non-personal references. No private trustee correspondence or unpublished minutes.

nodes.label: Kōrero feedback volume
nodes.description: Volume and quality of community feedback arriving through Giscus / GitHub Discussions across the Te Pā ecosystem.
intervention_candidates: Publish weekly aggregated feedback counts by domain
Route governance-tagged threads to a visible decision queue

nodes.label: Course revision rate
nodes.description: How often Kiwi Dialectic courses are revised in response to learner and community feedback.
intervention_candidates: Publish per-course changelog entries
Tag every course revision to the feedback thread that motivated it

nodes.label: Learner participation
nodes.description: Active participation across Te Pā and Kiwi Dialectic courses.
intervention_candidates: Publish anonymous participation counts per pathway
Track drop-off points and publish where onboarding breaks

nodes.label: Community trust in governance
nodes.description: Perceived legitimacy of Te Pā decisions among whānau, kaitiaki, and collaborators.
evidence_refs: [Māori Data Governance Model · Te Kāhui Raraunga](https://www.kahuiraraunga.io/)
intervention_candidates: Publish a 'you said / we changed' log after every governance cycle
Publish trustee decision notes in plain language

nodes.label: Kaitiaki seat legitimacy
nodes.description: Clarity and community recognition of kaitiaki authority within the trust structure.
evidence_refs: [Māori Data Governance Model · Te Kāhui Raraunga](https://www.kahuiraraunga.io/)
intervention_candidates: Publish kaitiaki role description and scope
Publish the appointment / rotation rule

nodes.label: Trustee review delay
nodes.description: Elapsed time between a decision entering trustee review and a published outcome.
intervention_candidates: Publish a rolling trustee turnaround indicator
Set a service-level target for governance decisions

nodes.label: Pathway autonomy
nodes.description: The degree to which learning and community pathways can make bounded decisions without trustee sign-off.
evidence_refs: [Māori Data Governance Model · Te Kāhui Raraunga](https://www.kahuiraraunga.io/)
intervention_candidates: Define the bounded powers of a pathway steward
Pilot one pathway with delegated authority and publish the outcome

nodes.label: Documentation clarity
nodes.description: How clearly the repo documents governance, contribution, and onboarding routes.
intervention_candidates: Publish a single canonical governance README
Add a CONTRIBUTING.md that names decision routes explicitly

nodes.label: Contributor confidence
nodes.description: Willingness of new contributors to open issues, discussions, or PRs.
intervention_candidates: Label 'good first issue' entries
Publish a public roadmap so newcomers can see where help is welcome

nodes.label: Māori control over rules
nodes.description: The degree to which Māori determine the governance rules of the ecosystem.
evidence_refs: [Māori Data Governance Model · Te Kāhui Raraunga](https://www.kahuiraraunga.io/)
intervention_candidates: Publish the rule set that formalises Māori control
Publish a review cadence for that rule set

nodes.label: Translation and bilingual capacity
nodes.description: Capacity to translate and publish bilingual (te reo Māori / English) content across the ecosystem.
intervention_candidates: Build a translation queue backed by Te Hiku Media resources
Publish which content is bilingual and which is English-only

nodes.label: Decision visibility
nodes.description: Whether decisions and the reasoning behind them are publicly visible to the community they affect.
intervention_candidates: Publish short plain-language decision notes
Tag every decision note to the discussion thread that informed it

nodes.label: New pathway growth
nodes.description: Rate at which new learning or language pathways can emerge inside the ecosystem.
intervention_candidates: Publish the criteria for launching a new pathway
Publish a template for pathway stewards

nodes.label: Moderation load
nodes.description: Effort required to keep community spaces safe and coherent.
intervention_candidates: Publish moderation queue turnaround indicators
Rotate moderation duty on a published schedule

nodes.label: Shared graph infrastructure
nodes.description: The technical substrate — repos, JSON, dashboards — that makes the ecosystem inspectable.
intervention_candidates: Publish a schema for governance-graph.json
Add JSON validation to the Actions workflow

loops.label: Community feedback improves course relevance
loops.story: More learner and community feedback drives better course revision, which lifts participation and generates more feedback.

loops.label: Trustee review protects legitimacy but slows response
loops.story: Formal review protects governance legitimacy, but long delays reduce trust and system responsiveness.

loops.label: Clear documentation grows contributor power
loops.story: Clearer docs increase contributor confidence, which strengthens the infrastructure and improves docs again.

loops.label: Pathway autonomy grows new language nodes
loops.story: When pathways have legitimate autonomy, communities are more likely to grow new nodes and trust the structure.
evidence_refs: [Māori Data Governance Model · Te Kāhui Raraunga](https://www.kahuiraraunga.io/)

loops.label: Moderation load can choke participation
loops.story: More community input is healthy, but if moderation and response capacity do not scale, decisions become less visible and participation can flatten.

decision_routes.label: Publish or revise a course
decision_routes.description: How a course revision moves from feedback to published change.

steps.action: Feedback lands in Kōrero (GitHub Discussions or Substack comments).
Kaimahi drafts a revision and opens a PR or Substack draft.
The changelog entry names the feedback thread that motivated the change.
A 'you said / we changed' note closes the loop with the community that raised the point.
evidence_refs: [Māori Data Governance Model · Te Kāhui Raraunga](https://www.kahuiraraunga.io/)

decision_routes.label: Change a governance rule
decision_routes.description: How a proposed change to a governance rule moves from proposal to ratified rule.

steps.action: A proposal is filed as a Discussions thread tagged 'governance'.
Kaitiaki review the proposal against the Māori Data Governance Model.
evidence_refs: [Māori Data Governance Model · Te Kāhui Raraunga](https://www.kahuiraraunga.io/)

steps.action: Trustees ratify or return the proposal within a published turnaround window.
The ratified rule is published in the repo and referenced in the docs.

decision_routes.label: Launch a new pathway
decision_routes.description: How a new learning or language pathway comes into being.

steps.action: A pathway steward proposes a new pathway with a scope and audience.
Translation and bilingual capacity is assessed against the pathway's needs.
The pathway is granted bounded authority to make its own content and process decisions.
evidence_refs: [Māori Data Governance Model · Te Kāhui Raraunga](https://www.kahuiraraunga.io/)

steps.action: The pathway's scope, steward, and boundaries are published in the repo.

snapshots.label: Current governance state
snapshots.blurb: Emphasises existing strengths, bottlenecks, and where feedback currently dies or slows down.

snapshots.label: Rhizome-integrated target
snapshots.blurb: Emphasises delegated pathways, visible information flow, stronger closure loops, and deeper self-organisation.

prompts: Which feedback channels produce actual changes in course content, governance rules, or repo structure?
Which decisions require trustee review, and which could be safely delegated to pathway-level kaitiaki?
Where are the longest delays: moderation, translation, trustee sign-off, publishing, or onboarding?
Which communities can currently see decisions being made, and which are expected to trust the process without visibility?
What conditions would allow a new language pathway to emerge without losing Māori control at the system core?
notes: Every node, loop, and route cites a public source. If no public source exists yet, the node is intentionally omitted rather than filled with a private reference.
The graph follows the same provenance rule as the observatory's indicators: public, aggregated, non-personal.
Meadows depth is attached to every node so interventions can be grouped by leverage tier.
This graph is a governance instrument, not dashboard theatre. Whānau, kaitiaki, and collaborators can challenge the map through Kōrero.
Next technical step: add JSON validation to the Actions workflow so schema drift is caught in CI.
